 {"id":"bd-koxf","title":"Define review plan artifact schema and validation","description":"# Task: Define Review Plan Artifact Schema and Validation

## Purpose
Define the machine-readable JSON schema that Claude must produce for each review session. This artifact is the contract between the Plan phase and Apply phase.

## Background: Why a Structured Artifact?
- Apply phase needs to know what to do
- Can't scrape agent transcripts reliably
- Enables resume after interruption
- Provides audit trail
- Enables metrics without parsing text

## File Location
```
{worktree}/.ru/review-plan.json
```

## Schema (Version 1)

```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "repo": "owner/repo",
  "worktree_path": "/home/user/.local/state/ru/worktrees/.../owner_repo",
  
  "items": [
    {
      "type": "issue",
      "number": 42,
      "title": "Authentication fails on Windows",
      "priority": "high",
      "decision": "fix",
      "notes": "Root cause: path separator in auth.py:234",
      "risk_level": "low",
      "files_changed": ["src/auth.py"],
      "lines_changed": 5
    },
    {
      "type": "pr",
      "number": 15,
      "title": "Add Redis caching",
      "priority": "normal",
      "decision": "skip",
      "notes": "Out of scope - adds external dependency",
      "risk_level": "n/a"
    }
  ],
  
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "q1",
      "prompt": "Should I refactor all path handling or just fix this case?",
      "options": [
        {"label": "Quick fix", "description": "Fix only auth.py (5 lines)"},
        {"label": "Full refactor", "description": "Modernize all paths (45 lines)"},
        {"label": "Skip", "description": "Not a priority"}
      ],
      "recommended": "Quick fix",
      "answered": true,
      "answer": "Quick fix",
      "answered_at": "2025-01-04T10:35:00Z"
    }
  ],
  
  "git": {
    "branch": "ru/review/20250104-103000-12345/owner-repo",
    "base_ref": "main",
    "commits": [
      {
        "sha": "abc123def456",
        "subject": "Fix Windows path handling in auth.py",
        "files": ["src/auth.py"],
        "insertions": 3,
        "deletions": 2
      }
    ],
    "tests": {
      "ran": true,
      "ok": true,
      "command": "make test",
      "output_summary": "12 tests passed",
      "duration_seconds": 45
    }
  },
  
  "gh_actions": [
    {
      "op": "comment",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "body": "Fixed in commit abc123. The issue was path separators on Windows..."
    },
    {
      "op": "close",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "reason": "completed"
    },
    {
      "op": "label",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "labels": ["fixed-in-main"]
    },
    {
      "op": "comment",
      "target": "pr#15",
      "body": "Thank you for the suggestion. After review, this is out of scope..."
    }
  ],
  
  "metadata": {
    "started_at": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
    "completed_at": "2025-01-04T10:45:00Z",
    "duration_seconds": 900,
    "context_usage_percent": 45,
    "model": "claude-sonnet-4",
    "driver": "local"
  }
}
```

## Field Definitions

### Top Level
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| schema_version | int | yes | Always 1 for this version |
| run_id | string | yes | Unique review run identifier |
| repo | string | yes | owner/repo format |
| worktree_path | string | yes | Absolute path to worktree |
| items | array | yes | Work items reviewed |
| questions | array | no | Questions asked/answered |
| git | object | no | Git state (may be empty if no changes) |
| gh_actions | array | no | GitHub actions to perform in Apply |
| metadata | object | yes | Session metadata |

### Item Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| type | "issue"\|"pr" | yes | Work item type |
| number | int | yes | Issue/PR number |
| title | string | yes | Item title |
| priority | string | yes | high/normal/low |
| decision | string | yes | fix/skip/needs-info/closed |
| notes | string | no | Explanation of decision |
| risk_level | string | no | low/medium/high/n/a |
| files_changed | array | no | Files modified for this item |
| lines_changed | int | no | Net lines changed |

### Question Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| id | string | yes | Unique question ID |
| prompt | string | yes | Question text |
| options | array | no | Available options |
| recommended | string | no | Agent's recommendation |
| answered | bool | yes | Whether answered |
| answer | string | no | Selected answer (if answered) |
| answered_at | string | no | ISO timestamp |

### gh_action Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| op | string | yes | comment/close/label/merge |
| target | string | yes | issue#N or pr#N |
| body | string | no | Comment body (for comment op) |
| reason | string | no | Close reason (for close op) |
| labels | array | no | Labels to add (for label op) |

## Validation Function
```bash
validate_review_plan() {
    local plan_file="$1"
    
    # Must exist
    [[ ! -f "$plan_file" ]] && { echo "Plan file not found"; return 1; }
    
    # Must be valid JSON
    if ! jq empty "$plan_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Invalid JSON"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Required top-level fields
    if ! jq -e '.schema_version and .repo and .items' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
        echo "Missing required fields: schema_version, repo, or items"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Schema version check
    local version
    version=$(jq -r '.schema_version' "$plan_file")
    if [[ "$version" != "1" ]]; then
        echo "Unsupported schema version: $version"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Items must have required fields
    if ! jq -e '.items | all(.type and .number and .decision)' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
        echo "Items missing required fields: type, number, or decision"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Validate decision values
    local invalid_decisions
    invalid_decisions=$(jq -r '.items[] | select(.decision | IN("fix","skip","needs-info","closed") | not) | .decision' "$plan_file")
    if [[ -n "$invalid_decisions" ]]; then
        echo "Invalid decision values: $invalid_decisions"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Validate gh_actions if present
    if jq -e '.gh_actions' "$plan_file" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if ! jq -e '.gh_actions | all(.op and .target)' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
            echo "gh_actions missing required fields: op or target"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
    
    echo "Valid"
    return 0
}
```

## Plan Summary
```bash
summarize_review_plan() {
    local plan_file="$1"
    
    jq -r '
        "Repository: \(.repo)",
        "Items reviewed: \(.items | length)",
        "  - Fixed: \([.items[] | select(.decision == "fix")] | length)",
        "  - Skipped: \([.items[] | select(.decision == "skip")] | length)",
        "  - Needs info: \([.items[] | select(.decision == "needs-info")] | length)",
        "Commits: \(.git.commits // [] | length)",
        "Tests: \(if .git.tests.ok then "PASS" else "FAIL/NOT RUN" end)",
        "gh_actions pending: \(.gh_actions // [] | length)"
    ' "$plan_file"
}
```

## Testing
- Validate correct plan passes
- Validate missing fields rejected
- Validate invalid decisions rejected
- Parse all field types correctly
- Handle optional fields gracefully

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema documented with all fields
- [ ] Validation function catches all errors
- [ ] Example plans provided for testing
- [ ] Summary function works
- [ ] Optional fields handled correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:01.268595285-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:42:10.057519193-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:42:10.057519193-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented validate_review_plan(), summarize_review_plan(), and get_review_plan_json_summary() functions in ru script. Added 14 unit tests in scripts/test_unit_review_plan.sh. All tests pass."}
 {"id":"bd-ldyv","title":"Implement quality gates framework (tests/lint before push)","description":"Task: Implement Quality Gates Framework

Purpose
-------
Run tests and lint before allowing push, ensuring code quality and
preventing broken commits from reaching the repository.

Background: Defense in Depth
----------------------------
AI can make mistakes. Quality gates catch:
- Syntax errors
- Test failures
- Lint violations
- Security issues (secrets in code)
- Build failures

Gate Types
----------

1. Test Gate
   Run project test suite
   Command: auto-detect or configured
   Failure: blocks push

2. Lint Gate
   Run linters/formatters
   Command: configured per-repo
   Failure: blocks push

3. Secret Scan Gate
   Check for exposed secrets
   Tool: gitleaks or regex fallback
   Failure: blocks push with warning

4. Build Gate (optional)
   Verify project builds
   Command: configured
   Failure: blocks push

Implementation
--------------

run_quality_gates()
  local wt_path="$1"
  local plan_file="$2"
  
  # Load per-repo policy
  local repo_id=$(jq -r .repo "$plan_file")
  local policy="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d/${repo_id//\//_}.conf"
  [[ -f "$policy" ]] && source "$policy"
  
  # Run lint if configured
  if [[ -n "$REVIEW_LINT_CMD" ]]; then
    log_step "Running lint: $REVIEW_LINT_CMD"
    if \! (cd "$wt_path" && eval "$REVIEW_LINT_CMD"); then
      log_error "Lint failed"
      return 1
    fi
  fi
  
  # Run tests
  run_test_gate "$wt_path" || return 1
  
  # Secret scanning
  run_secret_scan "$wt_path" || return 2  # 2 = needs review
  
  log_success "Quality gates passed"
  return 0

run_test_gate()
  local wt_path="$1"
  local test_cmd="${REVIEW_TEST_CMD:-}"
  
  # Auto-detect if not configured
  if [[ -z "$test_cmd" ]]; then
    if [[ -f "$wt_path/Makefile" ]] && grep -q "^test:" "$wt_path/Makefile"; then
      test_cmd="make test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/package.json" ]]; then
      test_cmd="npm test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/Cargo.toml" ]]; then
      test_cmd="cargo test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/setup.py" || -f "$wt_path/pyproject.toml" ]]; then
      test_cmd="pytest"
    fi
  fi
  
  if [[ -n "$test_cmd" ]]; then
    log_step "Running tests: $test_cmd"
    if \! (cd "$wt_path" && eval "$test_cmd"); then
      log_error "Tests failed"
      return 1
    fi
  fi
  
  return 0

run_secret_scan()
  local wt_path="$1"
  
  if command -v gitleaks &>/dev/null; then
    log_step "Scanning for secrets with gitleaks"
    if \! gitleaks detect --source "$wt_path" --no-git; then
      log_error "Secrets detected in changes"
      return 1
    fi
  else
    # Regex fallback
    if git -C "$wt_path" diff HEAD~1..HEAD | \
       grep -qiE "password\s*=|api.?key\s*=|secret\s*=|token\s*="; then
      log_warn "Potential secrets detected"
      return 2  # Needs human review
    fi
  fi
  
  return 0

Policy Configuration
--------------------
~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/owner_repo.conf:

REVIEW_TEST_CMD="make test"
REVIEW_LINT_CMD="npm run lint"
REVIEW_REQUIRE_TESTS=true
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true

Gate Results in Plan
--------------------
Update plan with gate results:
{
  "git": {
    "tests": {
      "ran": true,
      "ok": true,
      "command": "make test",
      "output_summary": "12 tests passed",
      "duration_seconds": 45
    }
  }
}

Testing
-------
- Verify auto-detection works
- Verify configured commands run
- Verify failures block push
- Verify secret scan catches patterns
- Test with missing tools

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Test gate runs before push
- [ ] Lint gate runs if configured
- [ ] Secret scan warns on patterns
- [ ] Failures block push with message
- [ ] Per-repo policies work
- [ ] Auto-detection works for common projects","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:44.686321504-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T18:21:11.827464852-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T18:21:11.827464852-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented quality gates framework with 8 functions: detect_test_command, detect_lint_command, run_test_gate, run_lint_gate, run_secret_scan, run_quality_gates, update_plan_with_gates. Supports auto-detection for Makefile, npm, Cargo, Python, Go, shell. Integrates with per-repo policies.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ldyv","depends_on_id":"bd-koxf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:14.491001528-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ldyv","depends_on_id":"bd-cutq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T16:10:25.431274346-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

All 41 assertions pass selftest.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m3s","depends_on_id":"bd-2rh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:09:08.591471625-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"bd-tcns","title":"Design agent prompts for review workflow","description":"Task: Design Agent Prompts for Review Workflow

Purpose
-------
Craft the prompts that instruct Claude on how to review issues/PRs, what
constraints to follow (no gh mutations), and what artifacts to produce.

Background: Prompt Engineering Matters
--------------------------------------
The quality of automated reviews depends heavily on prompt design:
- Clear policy communication (no PRs accepted)
- Explicit constraints (Plan mode = no mutations)
- Required output format (review-plan.json)
- Independent verification emphasis

Prompt Components
-----------------

1. System Context (AGENTS.md Reading)
   First read ALL of AGENTS.md and README.md carefully.
   Use your code investigation agent mode to fully understand the code,
   technical architecture, and purpose of the project. Use ultrathink.

2. Digest Handling
   If prior digest exists: read it, update based on delta commits
   If no digest: create comprehensive fresh digest
   Always write updated digest to .ru/repo-digest.md

3. Policy Communication
   POLICY: We do not accept PRs or outside contributions. The maintainer
   policy is disclosed to users. Claude reviews and independently decides
   whether and how to address submissions. Bug reports are welcome.

4. Review Instructions
   For each work item:
   - Read via gh issue view or gh pr view
   - Verify claims independently - do not trust user reports blindly
   - Check dates against recent commits (many issues may be stale)
   - If actionable: create local commits with fixes/features
   - If unclear: prepare question for maintainer

5. Plan Mode Constraints (CRITICAL)
   - DO NOT run gh issue comment, gh issue close, gh pr comment, etc.
   - DO NOT push any changes
   - Only use gh for READ operations (view, list)
   - All mutations will be applied by ru in a separate phase

6. Required Output
   Must produce .ru/review-plan.json with schema:
   {
     "schema_version": 1,
     "run_id": "...",
     "repo": "owner/repo",
     "items": [...],
     "questions": [...],
     "git": {...},
     "gh_actions": [...]
   }

Prompt Template Function
------------------------
generate_review_prompt() takes:
- wt_path: worktree path
- repo_name: owner/repo format
- work_items: JSON array of items to review
- run_id: unique review run ID

Checks for existing digest and adjusts instructions accordingly.

Prompt Variations
-----------------
- Quick Review: Focus on high-priority items, target 10 minutes
- Deep Review: Comprehensive analysis, architectural implications
- Security-Focused: Security items first, verify all claims

Testing
-------
- Verify Claude produces valid review-plan.json
- Verify gh mutations are NOT attempted
- Verify digest is created/updated
- Verify questions use AskUserQuestion tool

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Prompt template produces working reviews
- [ ] Plan mode constraints enforced
- [ ] Review plan artifact created correctly
- [ ] Policy communicated clearly
- [ ] Digest handling works both cases
- [ ] Questions formatted for aggregation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:17.313860817-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:44:47.540328214-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:44:47.540328214-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tcns","depends_on_id":"bd-koxf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:54.883229859-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vcr9","title":"Implement gh_actions execution from review plan","description":"Task: Implement gh_actions Execution

Purpose
-------
Execute GitHub mutations (comments, closes, labels) from the gh_actions
array in review-plan.json during the Apply phase.

gh_actions Schema
-----------------
{
  "gh_actions": [
    {"op": "comment", "target": "issue#42", "body": "Fixed in..."},
    {"op": "close", "target": "issue#42", "reason": "completed"},
    {"op": "label", "target": "issue#42", "labels": ["fixed-in-main"]},
    {"op": "comment", "target": "pr#15", "body": "Thank you..."}
  ]
}

Supported Operations
--------------------

1. comment
   Add comment to issue or PR
   gh issue comment N --body "..."
   gh pr comment N --body "..."

2. close
   Close issue or PR
   gh issue close N --reason "..."
   gh pr close N --comment "..."

3. label
   Add labels to issue
   gh issue edit N --add-label "label1,label2"

4. (merge - NOT SUPPORTED per policy)

Implementation
--------------

execute_gh_actions()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local plan_file="$2"
  
  local actions
  actions=$(jq -c .gh_actions[] "$plan_file" 2>/dev/null) || return 0
  
  while IFS= read -r action; do
    local op target
    op=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .op)
    target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
    
    case "$op" in
      comment) execute_comment "$repo_id" "$action" ;;
      close)   execute_close "$repo_id" "$action" ;;
      label)   execute_label "$repo_id" "$action" ;;
      *)
        log_warn "Unknown gh_action op: $op"
        ;;
    esac
  done <<< "$actions"

execute_comment()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local target body type number
  target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
  body=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .body)
  
  # Parse target: "issue#42" or "pr#15"
  type="${target%%#*}"
  number="${target##*#}"
  
  log_step "Commenting on $target"
  
  case "$type" in
    issue)
      gh issue comment "$number" -R "$repo_id" --body "$body"
      ;;
    pr)
      gh pr comment "$number" -R "$repo_id" --body "$body"
      ;;
  esac
  
  record_action_executed "$repo_id" "$action"

execute_close()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local target reason type number
  target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
  reason=$(echo "$action" | jq -r ".reason // \"completed\"")
  type="${target%%#*}"
  number="${target##*#}"
  
  log_step "Closing $target"
  
  case "$type" in
    issue)
      gh issue close "$number" -R "$repo_id" --reason "$reason"
      ;;
    pr)
      gh pr close "$number" -R "$repo_id" --comment "Closing: $reason"
      ;;
  esac
  
  record_action_executed "$repo_id" "$action"

execute_label()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local target labels_json type number
  target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
  labels_json=$(echo "$action" | jq -r ".labels | join(\",\")")
  type="${target%%#*}"
  number="${target##*#}"
  
  log_step "Adding labels to $target: $labels_json"
  
  gh issue edit "$number" -R "$repo_id" --add-label "$labels_json"
  
  record_action_executed "$repo_id" "$action"

Action Recording
----------------
Track what was executed for audit:

record_action_executed()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local log_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/gh_actions.log"
  local ts=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
  
  echo "${ts}|${repo_id}|${action}" >> "$log_file"

Error Handling
--------------
- Log errors but continue with other actions
- Record failed actions in state
- Retry logic for transient failures
- Skip already-executed actions on resume

Testing
-------
- Mock gh commands for unit tests
- Verify comment body escaping
- Verify close reasons work
- Verify labels applied
- Test error handling

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Comments posted correctly
- [ ] Issues/PRs closed with reason
- [ ] Labels applied
- [ ] Actions logged for audit
- [ ] Errors handled gracefully
- [ ] Idempotent on retry","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:42:09.158053493-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T18:32:04.738399665-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T18:32:04.738399665-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented gh_actions execution with 9 functions: execute_gh_actions, execute_gh_action_{comment,close,label}, gh_action_already_executed, record_gh_action_log, parse_gh_action_target, canonicalize_gh_action, get_gh_actions_log_file.  {"id":"bd-xcj6","title":"Implement JSON output mode for review command","description":"# Task: Implement JSON Output Mode

## Purpose
Add --json flag to `ru review` for machine-readable output, following the ru convention of structured stdout and human-readable stderr.

## Background: ru Output Convention
From AGENTS.md:
- stderr: All human-readable output (progress, errors, summary, help)
- stdout: Only structured output (JSON in --json mode, paths otherwise)

## JSON Output Schema

### Discovery Output (--dry-run --json)
```json
{
  "command": "review",
  "mode": "discovery",
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "timestamp": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
  "summary": {
    "repos_scanned": 50,
    "items_found": 23,
    "by_priority": {"critical": 2, "high": 5, "normal": 12, "low": 4},
    "by_type": {"issues": 18, "prs": 5}
  },
  "items": [
    {
      "repo": "owner/repo",
      "type": "issue",
      "number": 42,
      "title": "Bug in auth",
      "priority": "high",
      "score": 85,
      "labels": ["bug", "security"],
      "created_at": "2024-12-15T...",
      "updated_at": "2025-01-01T..."
    }
  ]
}
```

### Session Status Output (--json)
```json
{
  "command": "review",
  "mode": "plan",
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "status": "in_progress",
  "sessions": {
    "owner/repo1": {
      "state": "generating",
      "started_at": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
      "items_processing": 3
    },
    "owner/repo2": {
      "state": "waiting",
      "wait_reason": "ask_user_question",
      "question_id": "q123"
    }
  },
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "q123",
      "repo": "owner/repo2",
      "prompt": "Should I refactor?",
      "options": ["Yes", "No", "Skip"],
      "waiting_since": "2025-01-04T10:35:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "progress": {
    "sessions_total": 4,
    "sessions_complete": 1,
    "sessions_active": 2,
    "sessions_pending": 1,
    "questions_pending": 1,
    "questions_answered": 3
  }
}
```

### Completion Output (--json)
```json
{
  "command": "review",
  "mode": "plan",
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "status": "complete",
  "exit_code": 0,
  "summary": {
    "repos_reviewed": 4,
    "items_processed": 12,
    "items_fixed": 8,
    "items_skipped": 3,
    "items_needs_info": 1,
    "commits_created": 15,
    "questions_asked": 5,
    "duration_seconds": 847
  },
  "repos": {
    "owner/repo1": {
      "status": "complete",
      "plan_file": "/path/to/.ru/review-plan.json",
      "commits": 4,
      "items_processed": 3
    }
  }
}
```

### Apply Output (--apply --json)
```json
{
  "command": "review",
  "mode": "apply",
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "status": "complete",
  "applied": {
    "owner/repo1": {
      "pushed": true,
      "commits": 4,
      "gh_actions_executed": [
        {"op": "comment", "target": "issue#42", "success": true},
        {"op": "close", "target": "issue#42", "success": true}
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

## Implementation

### JSON Mode Flag
```bash
# In parse_review_args()
--json) REVIEW_JSON_OUTPUT=true ;;
```

### Output Router
```bash
review_output() {
    local type="$1"
    local data="$2"
    
    if [[ "$REVIEW_JSON_OUTPUT" == "true" ]]; then
        echo "$data"  # stdout for JSON
    else
        echo "$data" >&2  # stderr for human
    fi
}
```

### JSON Builders
```bash
build_discovery_json() { ... }
build_status_json() { ... }
build_completion_json() { ... }
build_apply_json() { ... }
```

## Integration Points
- Discovery phase outputs items JSON
- Session monitor outputs status JSON on refresh
- Completion outputs summary JSON
- Apply phase outputs actions JSON

## Testing
- Verify JSON is valid (jq parses it)
- Verify stderr has human output in --json mode
- Verify stdout is empty without --json
- Verify all schema fields present

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --json flag accepted
- [ ] Discovery JSON output correct
- [ ] Status JSON output correct
- [ ] Completion JSON output correct
- [ ] Apply JSON output correct
- [ ] Human output goes to stderr in JSON mode

 {"id":"bd-xdrt","title":"Phase 3: ntm Integration (Robot Mode, Activity Detection)","description":"Phase 3: ntm Integration

Overview
--------
Integrate with ntm (Named Tmux Manager) robot mode for advanced orchestration
capabilities: activity detection, health monitoring, and question routing.

Why ntm?
--------
ntm provides capabilities beyond basic tmux:
- Velocity-based activity detection (chars/sec)
- Health monitoring with error pattern detection
- Robot mode JSON API for programmatic control
- Workflow pipelines for complex multi-step tasks
- Session management and routing

Components
----------

3.1 ntm Driver Implementation
   Implement the unified driver interface using ntm robot mode API:
   - ntm --robot-spawn: Create sessions
   - ntm --robot-status: Query session state
   - ntm --robot-health: Monitor session health
   - ntm --robot-route: Get best pane for new work
   - ntm --robot-send: Deliver messages to sessions

3.2 Activity State Detection
   ntm detects Claude state via output velocity:
   - GENERATING: velocity > 10 chars/sec (active output)
   - WAITING: velocity < 1 chars/sec + prompt pattern
   - THINKING: low velocity, no prompt
   - STALLED: velocity == 0 for > 30 seconds
   - ERROR: error patterns detected

3.3 Wait Reason Extraction
   When WAITING, determine WHY:
   - ask_user_question: AskUserQuestion tool detected
   - agent_question_text: Question in plain text
   - external_prompt: Git/SSH/auth prompt
   - unknown: At prompt, reason unclear

3.4 Health Monitoring
   Monitor session health continuously:
   - Detect rate limits (429, quota exceeded)
   - Detect crashes (panic, SIGSEGV)
   - Detect stalls (no output for extended period)
   - Trigger recovery actions

3.5 Workflow Pipeline
   Define github-review.yaml workflow:
   - verify_prerequisites: Check gh auth
   - understand_codebase: Create/update digest
   - review_issues_prs: Main review work
   - finalize_artifacts: Validate plan created

3.6 Rate-Limit Governor
   Adaptive concurrency based on real limits:
   - Query GitHub API for remaining quota
   - Detect model rate limits from 429s
   - Adjust parallelism dynamically
   - Circuit breaker for cascading failures

Exit Criteria
-------------
- ntm driver passes all interface tests
- Activity detection works reliably
- Wait reasons extracted correctly
- Health monitoring catches common issues
- Rate-limit governor adjusts concurrency

Estimated Effort
----------------
~400 lines Bash + ~300 lines in ntm (Go)
Note: ntm changes are separate project","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:42.232591922-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T21:33:16.238846656-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T21:33:16.238846656-05:00","close_reason":"All ru-side components implemented:
- 3.1 ntm Driver: Full robot mode API (spawn, status, send, activity, health) at lines 5534-5810
- 3.2 Activity Detection: GENERATING/WAITING/THINKING/STALLED with velocity-based detection
- 3.3 Wait Reason Extraction: detect_wait_reason() with ask_user_question/external_prompt/agent_question_text
- 3.4 Health Monitoring: ntm driver reports health_monitoring=true, uses --robot-health
- 3.6 Rate-Limit Governor: Complete implementation (5869-6165) with circuit breaker, GitHub quota tracking, model rate limit detection

Note: 3.5 Workflow Pipeline (github-review.yaml) is explicitly ntm-side (Go) per phase description. Out of scope for ru.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xdrt","depends_on_id":"bd-5yy3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:26.666656889-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
